data.title: GeneDx falls after reporting Q4 and FY25 revenue
data.description:
• GeneDx reported full-year 2025 revenue of approximately $427M, inline with consensus of $427M, representing a 41% year-over-year increase and within its previously raised guidance range of $425 million to $428 million.

  • Exome and genome revenue was approximately $360M, reflecting 54% year-over-year growth (58% excluding a one-time 2024 benefit), in line with prior guidance of 53% to 55% growth.

  • Exome and genome test result volume totaled 97,271 for the year, an increase of 30.5% year over year, exceeding the company’s prior guidance of at least 30% growth.

  • Fourth-quarter 2025 revenue was approximately $121M, slightly higher than consensus of $120.5M, up 27% year over year.

  •  

    Exome and genome revenue in the quarter was approximately $104M, representing 32% year-over-year growth (43% excluding a one-time 2024 benefit).

     

  • Exome and genome test result volume reached 27,761 in the quarter, an increase of 34.3% year over year.

  • Full-year and fourth-quarter 2024 revenues and adjusted gross margin included a previously reported $6.8 million discrete benefit related to a multi-year appeal recovery from a single third-party payer. This benefit consisted of $5.8 million attributed to exome and genome revenue and $1.0 million attributed to other test lines in the fourth quarter of 2024.

  • Cash, cash equivalents, marketable securities, and restricted cash totaled approximately $172 million as of December 31, 2025.

  • The company issued full-year 2026 guidance expecting revenue between $540 million and $555 million ($531M consensus), including 33% to 35% growth in exome and genome revenue. Adjusted net income is expected to be positive.

  • Ms. Stueland will present at the 44th Annual J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference on January 14.

  • WGS -11.3% premarket to $119.99

  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Chesapeake Gold launches $10M bought deal public offering
data.description:
• Chesapeake Gold (CKG:CA) has entered into an agreement with Red Cloud Securities as lead underwriter and sole bookrunner, pursuant to which underwriters have agreed to purchase for resale 2.38M units of the company at a price of $4.20 per unit on a "bought deal" basis for gross proceeds of $10M.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share of the company and one-half of one common share purchase warrant. Each warrant will entitle the holder thereof to purchase one common share at a price of $5.65 at any time on or before that date which is 36 months following the closing date.
  • The Company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to advance the company's proprietary oxidative leach technology, the Metates project, the Lucy project and for general working capital.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Dexcom reports 13% rise in preliminary Q4 revenue, issues 2026 outlook
data.description:
• DexCom (DXCM) on Monday reported preliminary, unaudited results for the fourth quarter ended December 31, 2025 with total revenue of approximately $1.26 billion, up 13% from a year earlier. 
  • U.S. revenue is expected to be about $892 million, up 11%, while international revenue is seen roughly at $368 million, an increase of 18%, the company said.
  • For fiscal 2025, total preliminary, unaudited revenue is approximately $4.662 billion, up 16% from 2024 and reiterated its non-GAAP gross margin guidance of about 61% and operating margin guidance of 20% to 21%.
  • Looking ahead, the continuous glucose monitoring maker forecast 2026 revenue of $5.16 billion to $5.25 billion, implying growth of about 11% to 13%. It also expects non-GAAP gross margins of about 63% to 64% and operating margins of 22% to 23% for the year.
  • Dexcom will report its audited full fourth quarter and fiscal 2025 financial results on Thursday, February 12, 2026 after the close of market, followed by a conference call with management.
  • DXCM -0.0% premarket to $67.4.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Axsome Therapeutics announces prelim Q4, FY revenue above estimates
data.description:
• Axsome Therapeutics (AXSM) announced preliminary net product revenue of ~$196M for Q4 2025, representing 65% Y/Y growth, compared to estimate of $187M.
  • Full-year 2025 net product revenue is expected to be ~$638.5M, reflecting 66% growth Y/Y, vs estimate of $629.5M.
  • Revenue growth was driven by sales of AUVELITY, SUNOSI, and SYMBRAVO.

data.title: Adaptive Biotechnologies reports prelim Q4, FY revenue above estimate; shares up
data.description:
• Adaptive Biotechnologies (ADPT) reported preliminary revenue of ~$72M for Q4 2025, vs estimate of $59.4M and  revenue of $277M for full-year 2025 vs estimate of $264.7M.
  • The company reported cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities of ~$227M as of December 31, 2025.
  • ADPT shares up 12.6% premarket.

data.title: OrthoPediatrics sees FY25 revenue of $236.1M
data.description:
• OrthoPediatrics (KIDS) on Monday said posted record preliminary unaudited full year 2025 net revenue of $236.1 million, representing continued growth compared to $204.7 million in 2024 of 15%.
  • Preliminary full year domestic net revenue is expected to be $186.2 million, representing 16% annual growth and international net revenue is expected to be $49.9 million, representing 15% annual growth.
  • The company generated preliminary unaudited fourth quarter 2025 net revenue of $61.3 million, representing 16% growth compared to $52.7 million in the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Preliminary domestic fourth quarter net revenue is expected to be $48.4 million, representing 13% growth compared to the prior year period, and international net revenue is expected to be $12.9 million, representing 32% growth compared to the prior year period.
  • Consensus for FY25 amd Q4 revenues are $233.99M and $59.28M.
  • For Q4, it generated about $6M - $7M free cash flow.
  • 2026 Financial Guidance:OrthoPediatrics projects 2026 revenue to be in the range of $262 million to $266 million, representing 11% to 13% growth compared to full year 2025 preliminary unaudited net revenue. The company also expects approximately $10 million annual set deployment and expects to generate approximately $25 million Adjusted EBITDA in 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Digi Power X to buy $20 mln of Nvidia B300 GPUs for NeoCloudz AI platform
data.description:
• Digi Power X (DGXX) on Monday said it has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire approximately $20 million of next-generation NVIDIA B300 GPUs from Super Micro Computer.
  • The company said the GPUs will be deployed in its Tier III AI data centre infrastructure and offered to customers through NeoCloudz, with initial availability targeted for March 2026.
  • Digi Power X said the platform is intended to provide on-demand access to high-performance GPU computing for enterprises, AI startups and research institutions, without requiring customers to make large capital investments.
  • The company said it is targeting a positive return on invested capital within about 30 months of deployment, based on expected demand, pricing and utilisation, though this will depend on customer adoption and market conditions.
  • Digi Power X expects to begin onboarding initial customers in the first half of 2026 following completion of infrastructure deployment.
  • DGXX +0.35% premarket to $2.86.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: XOMA Royalty announces CFO transition
data.description:
• XOMA Royalty Corporation (XOMA) announced that chief financial officer Thomas Burns will step down.
  • The company has appointed Jeffrey Trigilio as its new CFO as part of the leadership transition.
  • Trigilio most recently served as chief financial and operating officer at Obsidian Therapeutics.
  • XOMA shares up 2% premarket.

data.title: Gold skies to new all-time high as Federal Reserve threat rattles markets
data.description:
Gold futures soared above $4,600/oz for the first time Monday, driven by investor concerns over threats to the independence of the U.S. Federal Reserve following Chairman Powell's revelation of a criminal indictment by U.S. prosecutors.

President Trump has long criticized Powell for what he calls an excessively cautious approach to monetary policy, and the investigation is viewed as a significant escalation in criticism against the Federal Reserve, raising fears that monetary policy could be influenced by political pressure and potentially reigniting worries about the U.S. dollar.

Gold's appeal also has been supported by rising geopolitical uncertainty as the U.S. capture of Venezuela's Maduro and violent protests in Iran reinforce the metal's safe-haven status; Trump is scheduled to be briefed Tuesday on options for responding to Iran, including possible military strikes.

Most-active Comex February gold (XAUUSD:CUR) +2.1% to $4,954.70/oz, and March silver (XAGUSD:CUR) +5.9% to $84.02/oz.

Precious metals equities are rising broadly pre-market, including Newmont (NEM) +2.7%, Barrick Mining (B) +2.8%, Agnico Eagle Mines (AEM) +2.5%, Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM) +2.7%, Kinross Gold (KGC) +3.1%, Iamgold (IAG) +2.9%, Anglogold Ashanti (AU) +4%, Gold Fields (GFI) +3.9%, Harmony Gold (HMY) +6.1%, Sibanye-Stillwater (SBSW) +5.7%, Hecla Mining (HL) +5.8%, Pan American Silver (PAAS) +4.2%, First Majestic Silver (AG) +5%, Endeavour Silver (EXK) +5.3%, Coeur Mining (CDE) +4.6%.

"With the Fed's independence now openly contested, the 'political risk' discount usually reserved for emerging markets is bleeding into the U.S. dollar, driving investors toward hard assets," OANDA analyst Zain Vawda said in a note.

Increased interference in the Fed could be a "bullish wildcard" for precious metals this year, although the reaction seems somewhat outsized, particularly for silver, Julius Baer’s Carsten Menke said in a note. "We believe that silver's outperformance over gold has become excessiv," Menke said, retaining a neutral view on the metal.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Predictmedix AI proposes name change to QScreen AI, citing focus on quantum-inspired healthcare AI
data.description:
• Predictmedix AI (CSE: PMED) (OTC Pink: PMEDF) said on Monday it plans to change its name to QScreen AI Inc and adopt the ticker symbol QAI, subject to regulatory approval, as part of a rebranding it says reflects a strategic focus on quantum-inspired artificial intelligence in healthcare.
  • The company said the proposed change is intended to highlight its use of quantum-inspired algorithms alongside traditional AI to analyze health data for screening, diagnostics and personalized care, while continuing to operate on standard computing hardware.
  • Predictmedix said it has collected more than 720,000 anonymized health scans through its SmartHealth AI Stations, which it uses to train and refine its models across applications including chronic disease detection, mental health assessment and infectious disease screening.
  • The name and ticker change are expected to take effect following approval by the Canadian Securities Exchange and other regulators.
  • PMEDF closed -4.85% at $0.0372.
  • Source: Press Release
